channel.title: Moorsalin's Blog
channel.copyright: Copyright © Moorsalin Munshi 2021. Powered by Hugo Dusk

item.title: About
item.description: Who am I My name is Moorsalin Munshi. I am interested in computer security, databases, geopolitics, and stock trading. I have a wide array of interests in addition to the listed items. I hope to get around to writing about my interests in an effort to consolidate my identity. What do I do I am currently employed as a Senior HEDIS/QARR analyst at EmblemHealth. In practice, that translates to data analyst.

item.title: Consuming Twitter API With Tweepy
item.description: Background Twitter is a veritable source of social media data. It is unhindered by the same privacy concerns as Facebook because of its cavalier attitude towards social networking and less intrusive data collection. Twitter has become quite popular amongst journalists since its inception, as well as politicians and other figures with political power. I am interested in the interactions journalists have on Twitter. There are two classes of users, blue checkmarks and non checkmarked.

item.title: Getting Started with Algorithmic Trading
item.description: Background In Summer 2019 I took a course on machine learning for trading. In the course, I learned that the general approach to algorithmic trading involves selecting some technical indicators and feeding them into machine learning models. A triggering signal is defined for buying or selling a stock. There are a few brokerages that provide APIs to their customers. I am using Alpaca, which provides API wrappers in Python, as well as Go, C#, and Javascript.

item.title: DIY Surveillance
item.description: Background I have a home surveillance kit I bought from Costco. It’s made by FLIR, who have a major market share in surveillance and optics in the US. The surveillance kit features software for viewing cameras, but its not very good.
